title: ferray-ufunc: no mixed-type operations (type promotion at ufunc level)
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

NumPy's ufunc type resolution (ufunc_type_resolution.c) automatically promotes operands: np.add(int32_arr, float64_arr) promotes the int32 to float64 before computation. ferray-ufunc requires both operands to have the same T. There's no way to add an Array<i32, D> and Array<f64, D> — it's a compile error. ferray-core has the promotion machinery (Promoted trait, PromoteTo, result_type), but ferray-ufunc doesn't use it. Add promoted variants that accept (Array<A,D>, Array<B,D>) where A: Promoted<B>, cast both to Output type, then compute.
